pattern: Only the count of zeros matters, and one operation moves that count to z-2i+k for some legal split, so the answer is a shortest path over zero-counts.
hints: The exact positions are irrelevant by symmetry; track only how many zeros remain.
Flipping i zeros and k-i ones changes the zero count to z minus 2i plus k, subject to availability bounds.
Run a breadth-first search over possible zero-counts from the start count down to zero.

edge_inputs.description: k larger than zero count needs detour
all ones but k forces flips back

stress.description: small binary strings so the exhaustive oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 11)))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [s, k]
